# HANDOFF — Remaining open items

Open items that need continuation context are listed below.

---

## Performance follow-ups — **DEFERRED, earmarked for `albert-2sandbox` branch** (added 2026-06-22)

These came out of the 2026-06-22 page-load performance review. The three lower-risk
items (RFQ init `forkJoin`, reference-data caching, per-row-expansion caching) were
**already implemented** on the working branch — see `docs/performance-caching.md`.
The items below were intentionally **not** done yet; do them on `albert-2sandbox`.

### A. Server-side pagination for the RFQ `getAll` query — **DESIGNED (2026-06-23), pending developer review**

> 📄 **Full implementation design: [`docs/rfq-ssrm-migration-plan.md`](docs/rfq-ssrm-migration-plan.md)** —
> a complete cross-repo (backend + frontend) SSRM-with-server-side-grouping plan with the field
> map, filter/sort translators, Res + merch-group filterability, group/leaf query design,
> full-dataset export, file list, test plan, risks, and open questions for the reviewer.
> Product-owner decisions baked in: keep server-side grouping; Res + merch-groups must be
> filterable (carton dims need not be); export the full filtered set. **Not yet implemented** —
> review the doc before building; ship behind the dual-endpoint so `/getAllItem` stays as rollback.

**Problem:** the RFQ grid's backend `getAll` (`designflow-backend/models/lib.model.js`,
`Customer.getAll`) does an unbounded `findAll` across ~10 joined tables and returns
**all** rows (currently 3000+). It's the heaviest query behind the page.

**⚠️ The critical caveat (why this is deferred):** the RFQ grid currently uses AG
Grid's **client-side row model** — all rows are in the browser, so filtering and
sorting work across the *entire* dataset for free. If you naïvely add `LIMIT/OFFSET`
to the backend **without** also moving to AG Grid's **server-side row model**, then
filter/sort will only act on the one page currently loaded — i.e. **filtering would
silently stop working across all 3000+ rows.** That is a functional regression, not
just a perf change.

**Correct approach (the whole job):**
1. Switch the RFQ grid to the server-side row model (SSRM) — see how
   `itemLibrary` / `prod_tracking` already do this (they use SSRM today).
2. Push **filtering, sorting, and pagination to the backend** so they operate over
   the full set, and have `getAll` accept `startRow/endRow/filterModel/sortModel`.
3. Return a total row count for the grid.
4. Re-verify: Saved Views, the Division logic, default Step filter, master/detail
   expansion, export (must export the full filtered set, not just the loaded page),
   and cell notes.

**Do not ship a bare `LIMIT` on `getAll`.** Either do the full SSRM migration or leave it.

### B. Adopt `ChangeDetectionStrategy.OnPush` + precompute template getters

**Problem:** only ~8 of ~278 components use OnPush. Grid pages (rfq, itemLibrary,
prod_tracking, etc.) use default change detection with method/getter bindings that
re-run every CD cycle (e.g. `valueGetter` doing `allMerchGroups.find(...)` per cell,
template getters that rebuild arrays).

**Approach (incremental, per-component — do NOT flip globally):**
- Convert one heavy component at a time to `OnPush`; drive updates through the
  already-injected `ChangeDetectorRef` (`markForCheck()`), and use immutable updates
  (new array/object references) instead of in-place mutation.
- Precompute template getter results into fields when the source data changes.
- **Risk:** OnPush changes *when* the view updates — anything relying on mutate-in-place
  will silently stop refreshing. Each component needs manual testing after conversion.

### C. N+1 / row-by-row writes → `bulkCreate` — **AUDIT-FIRST process defined (2026-06-23)**

> 📄 **Audit template + staged process + hotspot inventory:
> [`designflow-data-syncing/docs/bulkcreate-migration-audit.md`](../designflow-data-syncing/docs/bulkcreate-migration-audit.md)**
> (lives in the repo where the work happens). It defines a per-hotspot fill-in audit checklist and a
> staged process: **(0) fix error visibility → (1) per-hotspot audit incl. the conditional-update
> decision → (2) verify/add the DB unique constraint → (3) baseline + tests + scheduler validation
> in non-prod → (4) chunked `bulkCreate` in a transaction → (5) parity check → next service.**
> Migrate **one service at a time**; first service is a pattern-setter (merch-group sync), NOT the
> riskiest (`getProdOrderDetail`).

This is primarily a **backend / sync-service** concern, listed here so the frontend
handoff points at it. Row-by-row `INSERT`/`UPDATE` loops (often with a per-row
`SELECT` first) should become batched `bulkCreate(rows, { updateOnDuplicate: [...] })`
or batched `findAll({ where: { [Op.in]: keys } })`. Hotspots identified:
`designflow-data-syncing` sync loops (e.g. `getProdOrderDetail`, merch-group sync,
prepack associations) and `designflow-tracking` `Box.addSamples`.

**Why "investigate further", not "just do it":**
- Each site needs a real **unique constraint** + an explicit `updateOnDuplicate`
  column list — wrong list either clobbers kept data or silently skips updates.
- Some loops do **conditional** updates ("only if incoming `modTime` is newer") that
  `updateOnDuplicate` can't express without a SQL `CASE`/literal — naive batching can
  resurrect stale data.
- Per-row **hooks/side-effects** (Teams notifications, audit writes) are skipped by
  `bulkCreate` unless `individualHooks: true` (which removes most of the speed gain).
- Watch Postgres bound-parameter limits (~65k) → cap batch size; wrap multi-step
  writes in a transaction for atomicity.

Action: audit each hotspot for the four points above, then migrate service-by-service
with the upsert keys verified against existing data first.

---

## Item Library redesign — **IMPLEMENTED, COMMITTED & PUSHED** (updated 2026-06-21)

The Item Library page (`pages/itemLibrary`) was re-skinned to the Item Detail
token palette and extended with image-forward layouts. Committed and pushed to
`sandbox-albert`; PR `sandbox-albert → develop` open.

### What shipped

- Single consolidated **command bar** (replaces the multi-row `mat-toolbar`) with
  a **Display** popover for Layout / View / Density / Columns.
- Three new AG-Grid cell renderers: `ItemThumbnailComponent` (`itemThumbnail`),
  `StatusPillComponent` (`statusPill`), `PropertyCellComponent` (`propertyCell`).
- `SplitRailComponent` (persistent 372px preview rail) and `GalleryComponent`
  (card-grid view) over the same server-side datasource.
- Runtime toggles: Filmstrip ↔ Split, Table ↔ Gallery, Comfortable ↔ Compact.
- Default visible columns: 11 of 13 (Reference # and Design # hidden).
- Shared pure helpers in `item-library-shared.ts` (status tones, property hues,
  rail fields) with full Jest coverage; each new component has a behavioral spec.

### Reference

- `docs/item-library-redesign.md` — full component/token/helper reference.
- Server-side row model, saved views, Division logic, export, cell notes, and
  the archive context menu are unchanged.

### Follow-ups (not blocking)

- Pre-existing `notesDataSource` TS error in `gridOptions` is shared by all grid
  pages (rfq, art-piece, licensing/prod tracking) and predates this work.
- Below ~1024px the Split rail should collapse to a drawer (not yet built).

---

## AG Grid Theming API migration — **PLANNED, not started** (added 2026-06-29)

> 📄 **Full implementation plan: [`fix_theming_api.md`](fix_theming_api.md)** — phased
> migration of every grid from the legacy `ag-theme-alpine` CSS theme onto AG Grid v36's
> **Theming API**, with the current-state wiring map, file/scope inventory, what breaks vs.
> survives in the custom CSS, per-grid QA checklist, RFQ-specific guidance, rollback, and an
> effort estimate (~1 day code + 1–2 days visual QA).

**Why this exists:** after the AG Grid v36 upgrade every grid rendered **blank** (a theming
default change, **not** the license — the "Invalid License" banner is known noise). The fix
(commits `8b5d0d5`, `3f75198`, `dbb2722`) pinned all grids to the **legacy** theme + loaded
v36's own legacy CSS, which works today and preserves the redesign's `ag-theme-alpine`
styling. That, however, reverses the Theming-API intent documented in `AGENTS.md` §11. The
plan migrates everything onto the Theming API (the half-built `GRID_THEME_CONFIG` /
`GridThemeEventsService` / theme-picker infra already exists) and reconciles §11.

**Not urgent:** legacy CSS still ships and works in v36; do this as a deliberate standalone
project (or when a future AG Grid major finally drops legacy CSS). Recommend a Phase-0 spike
on the Item Library grid first to calibrate per-grid effort.

---

```js
  try {
    await NotificationService.createManyNotifications(targets, {
      type: 'task_assignment',  // or 'status_change', 'approval'
      event: 'assigned',
      title: 'Task assigned',
      message: `${authorName} assigned a task to you`,
    });
  } catch {}
});
```

**Where to wire it:**
- Task assignment: `services/task.service.js` or wherever `createTask` / `assignTask` lives
- RFQ status change: `routes/lib.router.js` PATCH handler for `rfq_status`
- Licensing approval: `routes/lib.router.js` PATCH handler for `licensing_status`

**Frontend icon map already in place** (`notification.router.ts` maps `type` → icon/color):
| `type` | Icon |
|---|---|
| `mention` | alternate_email |
| `task_assignment` | assignment_ind |
| `approval` | check_circle |
| anything else | notifications |

---

## 3. RAG step 1: keyword search over hts-rates.json (backend — designflow-backend)

Add a `/api/admin/hts-rates/search?desc={text}` endpoint that does a simple keyword match
over description fields in `data/hts-rates.json`. Inject the top 5 matches into the AI's
initial message as "Potentially relevant HTS headings." Zero new infrastructure — uses the
already-bundled USITC JSON file.

**Why it matters:** The system prompt covers 9 chapters. For products outside those chapters,
the model falls back on training data. A simple keyword search would inject the exact USITC
subheadings the model should consider, improving accuracy on edge-case products.

**Implementation sketch:**
- Backend: `helpers/hts-rates.js` — add `searchByDescription(text, limit = 5)` that filters
  `hts-rates.json` entries whose `description` field contains any token from `text`.
- Route: `routes/admin.router.js` — `GET /api/admin/hts-rates/search?desc={text}`
- Frontend: `ai-settings.service.ts` — add `searchHtsRates(desc: string)` method
- Inject in `initAiSession()` alongside CROSS results (non-fatal if empty or missing)

---

## Licensing tracking ↔ poppim PM integration — Phase-0 bridge (updated 2026-06-14)

**Status:** shared-key verification **DONE (GO, 99.3%)**; Phase-0 bridge **designed, not yet built**.

**Context:** See `docs/licensing-tracking-poppim-integration.md` (analysis + verified result) and
`docs/licensing-poppim-phase0-bridge.md` (the build spec). Albert's direction (2026-06-13): DesignFlow
stays the system of record for licensing approval; poppim (Directus app at `pm.designflow.app`) consumes a
**read-only** copy.

**Done (this session):**
- Confirmed both data models; the two run on separate clouds/DBs (API↔API, no shared DB).
- **Verified the join key (GO):** DesignFlow `item_num_id` ⇄ the item-number prefix of poppim
  `product.name` (NOT `product.code`). **9,224 / 9,293 (99.3%)** poppim item-nos match DesignFlow; misses
  are truncated codes. Matches are almost entirely licensed (div 1/2/8). Key is **not** unique on
  `item_num_id` alone → use `(item_num_id, compan_code_fk, div_code_fk)`.
- Wrote the Phase-0 bridge spec (read-only, one-way DesignFlow→poppim status mirror).

**Next action (build the bridge — see `docs/licensing-poppim-phase0-bridge.md`):**
1. Collect the 4 open inputs (spec §8): export the poppim `stage` collection; run
   `SELECT id, phase, status, "order" FROM designflow."LicenseFeedBacks" ORDER BY "order";`; obtain a
   Directus service-account write token; pick hosting (Cloud Run job vs Directus Flow).
2. Add the read-only `df_*` fields + backfill `product_link.linked_external_id` in the `u2giants/directus`
   schema repo.
3. Build the bulk read endpoint on DesignFlow (`/lic/status-export`) and the idempotent sync job.
4. Dry-run/log-only one cycle, spot-check 20 products, then enable writes.

**Risks / watchouts:**
- Key needs the `(item_num_id + company + division)` composite (`item_num_id` alone has ~1,500 DF
  collisions; 367 poppim products share an item-no). `compan_code_fk` is often NULL — tolerate it.
- ~42% of poppim "products" are non-product ClickUp rows (comments / routing notes / presentations) — the
  bridge must filter these, not treat every poppim product row as a SKU.
- The bridge is **API↔API across GCP↔Coolify**; there is no shared DB. Do not assume a SQL join is possible.

---

## RFQ session 2026-06-15 — redesign reverted; backend develop-sync reverted

Status: done (everything reverted/shipped); a future develop re-sync needs care.

- All RFQ polish + redesign work this session was reverted on `sandbox-albert` (frontend revert commit `268e04ef`). It was reverted (not force-removed) to preserve the parallel Item Detail redesign commit (`c9b22f95`) that landed on top.
- The **only kept change** is the `index.html` cache fix (`7e9ca2d7`, `nginx.conf`) — see `AGENTS.md §14` and `docs/deployment.md → Static asset caching`.
- The backend `develop → sandbox-albert` merge was also reverted (`designflow-backend` `087fdc5`). **Re-syncing develop later needs care:** reverting a *merge commit* means a plain `git merge develop` won't re-bring it — see `designflow-backend/AGENTS.md §14`. Land the cache fix to prod first so the lazy-loading rollout doesn't re-break stale browsers.
- If the RFQ visual redesign is revisited, it MUST go through the AG Grid Theming API, not CSS overrides — see `AGENTS.md §11 "The RFQ grid uses AG Grid's Theming API"`. A corrected design brief was drafted in-session but not committed.

---

Delete this file once all items above are resolved.

input.old_string:
## AG Grid Theming API migration — **PLANNED, not started** (added 2026-06-29)

> 📄 **Full implementation plan: [`fix_theming_api.md`](fix_theming_api.md)** — phased
> migration of every grid from the legacy `ag-theme-alpine` CSS theme onto AG Grid v36's
> **Theming API**, with the current-state wiring map, file/scope inventory, what breaks vs.
> survives in the custom CSS, per-grid QA checklist, RFQ-specific guidance, rollback, and an
> effort estimate (~1 day code + 1–2 days visual QA).

**Why this exists:** after the AG Grid v36 upgrade every grid rendered **blank** (a theming
default change, **not** the license — the "Invalid License" banner is known noise). The fix
(commits `8b5d0d5`, `3f75198`, `dbb2722`) pinned all grids to the **legacy** theme + loaded
v36's own legacy CSS, which works today and preserves the redesign's `ag-theme-alpine`
styling. That, however, reverses the Theming-API intent documented in `AGENTS.md` §11. The
plan migrates everything onto the Theming API (the half-built `GRID_THEME_CONFIG` /
`GridThemeEventsService` / theme-picker infra already exists) and reconciles §11.

**Not urgent:** legacy CSS still ships and works in v36; do this as a deliberate standalone
project (or when a future AG Grid major finally drops legacy CSS). Recommend a Phase-0 spike
on the Item Library grid first to calibrate per-grid effort.

---
input.new_string:
## AG Grid Theming API migration — **✅ DONE, shipped & verified** (completed 2026-06-30)

Migrated every grid from the legacy `ag-theme-alpine` CSS theme onto AG Grid v36's
**Theming API**. Shipped on `sandbox-albert`, deployed to the sandbox, and visually verified.

**Commits:** `ffd28c92` (theming) · `edcd8903` + `a19055d9` (a pre-existing cell-notes build
break that surfaced en route — see below) · `7927535f` (docs + dead-code cleanup).

**What was done (≈5 files, not the ~35 the plan estimated):**
- `grid-themes.config.ts`: `APP_GRID_THEME` = `themeAlpine.withParams({ fontFamily: Hanken })`
  + `columnDropStyleBordered`/`buttonStyleQuartz` parts; `APP_GRID_THEME_DARK` for dark mode.
- `ag-grid-modules.ts`: global default `theme: 'legacy'` → `APP_GRID_THEME`.
- `grid-ui.service.ts` `bootstrapTheme()`: applies the dark theme object in dark mode instead
  of forcing `'legacy'`.
- `styles.scss` + `angular.json`: removed the legacy `ag-grid.css` / `ag-theme-alpine.css`
  imports (loading them with the Theming API → error #106 / blank grids).
- **Key shortcut:** the `.ag-theme-alpine` class was **kept** on grid elements as a plain
  styling hook, so the ~17 custom SCSS files kept applying unchanged. This is why scope was ~5
  files. See `AGENTS.md` §"All grids use AG Grid's Theming API" and `fix_theming_api.md`.

**Verified:** all builds green; console clean of `error #106` / `warning #9`;
`AG Grid: initialised successfully`; Item Library, RFQ (groups + frozen Step column), and
Licensing render correctly on the live sandbox.

**Two ⚠️ gotchas worth knowing (see also `AGENTS.md`):**
1. **Local node_modules drift** — the dev box had AG Grid **35.2.1** installed while
   `package.json`/`yarn.lock` pin **36.0.0**, so local `tsc`/`jest` passed against the wrong
   (looser) types while Cloud Build failed. Always `yarn install --immutable` to match the
   lockfile before trusting local checks. Burned two Cloud Build cycles.
2. **Pre-existing cell-notes build break** — `rfq.component.ts` was committed using
   `this.rfqCellNotes.gridOptions`, but the type was only in uncommitted local changes, so the
   branch had been red since `5e59c243` (sandbox stuck at `834d7a2`). Landed the cell-notes
   cluster + made `DesignflowNotesDataSource.getNote/setNote` required so it fits AG Grid 36's
   `notesDataSource` type. **This resolves the "pre-existing `notesDataSource` TS error"**
   follow-up noted under the Item Library redesign section above.

**Remaining (optional, not blocking):** dark-mode grid polish (falls back to a dark base —
was already light-only pre-migration); the toolbar theme picker is still inert (re-enable or
hide). No outstanding work to ship.

---

file.content:
## 4a. Branch, sync, test, and PR workflow

All feature work for this sandbox happens on `sandbox-albert`.

Before adding new code:
1. Confirm the local branch is `sandbox-albert`.
2. Fetch GitHub.
3. Bring the latest `origin/develop` into GitHub's `sandbox-albert` by merging `origin/develop` into local `sandbox-albert`, then pushing `sandbox-albert` to GitHub.
4. Pull GitHub's `sandbox-albert` back into local `sandbox-albert`.
5. Only then add or change code.

Branch rules:
- Work only on `sandbox-albert`.
- Use `develop` only as the source branch for syncing and the PR base.
- Never commit to, merge into, or open PRs against `main`.
- Do not force-push.

Quality gate:
- Add or update unit tests for every function or behavior you create or change.
- Run the relevant frontend tests before committing; prefer the narrowest focused tests plus a TypeScript/build check when templates or services change.

PR rule:
- Commit locally on `sandbox-albert`, then push to `origin/sandbox-albert`.
- Keep the GitHub PR for `sandbox-albert` -> `develop` updated after pushing.
- If the PR does not exist, create it with `develop` as the base and `sandbox-albert` as the head.

Commit identity:
- Git `user.name`/`user.email` are intentionally unset in this repo. Set them per commit and use the GitHub **noreply** email — history is authored as `Albert Hazan <u2giants@users.noreply.github.com>`:
  `git -c user.name="Albert Hazan" -c user.email="u2giants@users.noreply.github.com" commit ...`
- Pushing a commit authored with `u2giants@gmail.com` or `albert@popcre.com` is rejected with `GH007: Your push would publish a private email address` (account email-privacy protection). Recover with `git -c user.email="u2giants@users.noreply.github.com" commit --amend --reset-author --no-edit`, then push.

---

Quality gate:
- Add or update unit tests for every function or behavior you create or change.
- Run the relevant frontend tests before committing; prefer the narrowest focused tests plus a TypeScript/build check when templates or services change.
- **⚠️ Match the lockfile before trusting local checks.** Local `node_modules` can drift from `package.json`/`yarn.lock` (observed 2026-06-30: AG Grid **35.2.1** installed locally while the repo pins **36.0.0**). Looser local types let `tsc`/`jest` pass while the Docker build (`yarn test` on a fresh install) fails — i.e. green-locally / red-in-CI. Run `yarn install --immutable` to sync, especially after a dependency bump, before relying on any local `tsc`/`jest` result. The Docker build runs `yarn test`, so a clean local install is the only faithful CI reproduction.

Here's my honest read. Technically, **yes — it's safe and cleanly separable.** The e2e files are one self-contained group (fixtures, page objects, specs, utils), they're automation-only (the login page object even says "not part of the application bundle"), and they don't affect the `yarn test`/Docker build or deploy at all. They also don't depend on the hts/ai-settings/itemDetail work, so I can commit just them without entangling anything else.

**But one caveat worth your call first:** the timestamps show an **active concurrent session** progressively editing this repo today —

| File group | Last edited |
|---|---|
| e2e (fixtures/utils/sample-tracking) | 16:36 |
| hts-classifier | 16:45 |
| ai-settings | 17:16 |
| (untracked e2e pages/specs) | yesterday 06-29, stable |

That 16:36→16:45→17:16 progression looks like one session working through e2e → HTS → AI-settings as a batch. If that's another agent (your memory flags a past concurrency incident) or you intend to commit all of it together as one feature push, me committing e2e/* alone could fragment their intended grouping or collide.

So my recommendation:
- **If the e2e work is yours and ready, and no other session is about to commit it as part of a bigger batch** → I'll commit the 10 e2e files (4 modified + 6 untracked) as a clean `test(e2e): ...` commit and leave hts/ai-settings/itemDetail untouched. Low risk.
- **If you're unsure** → safest to leave it; it's harmless uncommitted and doesn't block anything.

Want me to go ahead and commit just the e2e/* files? If yes, I'll also do a quick coherence check (that the untracked specs reference the page objects correctly) before committing.
